title: indulgent aerated portuguese red bean paste pastry
description: a rich, airy pastry filled with anko-red-bean-paste and infused with ayurvedic sattvic oil
instructions: preheat the oven to 400 degrees fahrenheit (200 degrees celsius). line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
in a medium bowl, whisk together the gluten-free flour, tapioca starch, and granulated sugar.
add the softened butter to the dry ingredients and mix until a crumbly dough forms.
stir in the anko-red-bean-paste and mix until well combined.
roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/4 inch (6 mm).
cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
place a tablespoon of the anko-red-bean-paste mixture in the center of each dough circle.
brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg and fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle.
press the edges together to seal the pastry and use a fork to crimp the edges.
brush the tops of the pastries with the infused-oil and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar.
bake the pastries for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
allow the pastries to cool on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes before serving.
